title: [ImportVerilog] Support `$` literal within queue indexing expressions
body:
This PR adds support for the `$` literal when indexing a queue. The following strategy has been used:
- Add a field `currentQueue` in `Context` which is set to the queue within the current index expression, if any.
- When encountering an `UnboundedLiteral`, generate `QueueSizeBIOp` and subtract 1 to get the last element.
The behaviour of `$` on an empty queue is not specified, as far as I can find. For now it returns index `-1`, which is out of bounds.

The `QueueSizeBIOp` has also been modified to take the queue by value instead of as a reference. This avoids ugly code in `ElementExpressionHandler`, which currently (and reasonably) dereferences the variable being indexed if the operation is a read. I think taking the queue by-value makes the most sense, since it never mutates the queue. Arguably, changing the queue to be a reference was a mistake I introduced in #9651.
